$schema_note: Destinations dataset for Wonder Travel Car Rental — Masvingo Province, Zimbabwe. Image URLs are sourced from Wikimedia Commons (CC-licensed, stable) where available, and from reputable Zimbabwean tourism / news sources otherwise. The build process MUST: (1) re-host every image locally under /public/images/destinations/{slug}/ before launch, (2) verify each image still loads and respect each source's licence, (3) replace any image flagged with "verify": true after a manual visual check. Do NOT hot-link these URLs in production.

destinations.name: Great Zimbabwe National Monument
destinations.summary: UNESCO World Heritage Site and the country's namesake. Ancient stone city built by the Shona between the 11th and 15th centuries — Hill Complex, Great Enclosure, and Valley Ruins.
destinations.longDescription: Great Zimbabwe is the largest ancient stone structure in sub-Saharan Africa, covering ~800 hectares about 30 km south-east of Masvingo city. The Conical Tower inside the Great Enclosure and the dramatic granite walls of the Hill Complex are the headline sights. Allow at least three hours; arrive at opening (06:00) to beat the heat.

images.alt: Aerial view of the Great Zimbabwe ruins looking west
Wall of the Great Enclosure at Great Zimbabwe
Conical Tower inside the Great Enclosure

destinations.name: Gonarezhou National Park
destinations.summary: Zimbabwe's second-largest national park (5,053 km²). Shona for 'Place of Elephants'. Home to baobabs, palm forests, large herds of elephant, lions, wild dogs, black and white rhino.
destinations.longDescription: Gonarezhou is Zimbabwe's wildest, least-visited big-game park — a frontier landscape on the Mozambique border. Famous for its giant baobabs, the Runde and Save river systems, big elephant herds, and the dramatic Chilojo Cliffs. Best explored over 3–5 days with a 4x4.

images.alt: Elephant herd in Gonarezhou National Park

destinations.name: Chilojo Cliffs
destinations.summary: Iconic red sandstone cliffs inside Gonarezhou, towering over the Runde River. One of Zimbabwe's most photographed natural landmarks.

images.alt: Chilojo Cliffs above the Runde River

destinations.name: Lake Mutirikwi (Lake Kyle)
destinations.summary: Zimbabwe's second-largest dam after Tugwi-Mukosi. ~90 km² lake formed by the Kyle Dam (1958–1961) on the Mutirikwi River. Boating, fishing, sundowners.

images.alt: Lake Mutirikwi with Kyle Dam wall in distance

destinations.name: Lake Mutirikwi Recreational Park (Kyle Game Park)
destinations.summary: 8,000-hectare game park around Lake Mutirikwi. Home to three of the Big Five (buffalo, leopard, white rhino), plus giraffe, zebra, wildebeest. Self-guided drives.

images.alt: White rhino at Lake Mutirikwi Recreational Park

destinations.name: Tugwi-Mukosi Dam (Tokwe-Mukosi)
destinations.summary: Zimbabwe's largest inland dam (1.8 billion m³). 90.3 m concrete-face rock-fill wall at the confluence of the Tugwi and Mukosi rivers, completed 2017.
destinations.longDescription: About 72–115 km south of Masvingo, Tugwi-Mukosi is the country's biggest dam outside of Kariba. A planned game park sits in the buffer zone, and lakeside lodges are being developed. Already a fishing, boating and birdwatching destination.

destinations.name: Save Valley Conservancy
destinations.summary: ~3,100 km² private conservancy — one of the largest in Africa. All Big Five present. Premium safari lodges including Sango and Chishakwe.

destinations.name: Malilangwe Wildlife Reserve
destinations.summary: Premier private reserve managed by the Malilangwe Trust. Birding paradise, antelope, buffalo, leopard. Home of the Singita Pamushana luxury lodge.

destinations.name: Hippo Valley Estates
destinations.summary: Vast sugar-cane plantation on the Runde River — anchor of the Lowveld economy. Hippo Valley Country Club, mill tours, and irrigation landscape.

images.alt: Sugarcane fields at Hippo Valley Estates

destinations.name: Triangle Estates
destinations.summary: Sister sugar plantation to Hippo Valley, also producing ethanol. Triangle Country Club is a long-standing Lowveld social hub.

destinations.name: Mkwasine Estate
destinations.summary: Third major sugar estate alongside Hippo Valley and Triangle. Working agricultural landscape with irrigation infrastructure.

destinations.name: Runde (Lundi) River
destinations.summary: The Lowveld's lifeline river. Hippos, crocodiles, waterbirds — accessible right from Chiredzi town's bridge.

destinations.name: Chilo Gorge
destinations.summary: Dramatic gorge on the Save River bordering Gonarezhou. Home of Chilo Gorge Safari Lodge.

images.alt: Aerial view of Chilo Gorge on the Save River

destinations.name: Buffalo Range Airport
destinations.summary: The Lowveld's domestic airport — gateway to Gonarezhou and Save Valley. Recently refurbished.

destinations.name: Boli Mhlanguleni Multi-Cultural Village
destinations.summary: Multi-cultural village in Chiredzi south. Hosts the annual Great Limpopo Cultural Trade Fair (Zimbabwe / Mozambique / South Africa).

destinations.name: Chipinda Pools
destinations.summary: Northern entrance and main camp area for Gonarezhou. Self-catering chalets, river views, big-elephant country.

destinations.name: Chiredzi Town Market
destinations.summary: Vibrant local trade hub — fresh produce, roasted maize, second-hand clothes, and small handcrafted souvenirs.

destinations.name: Sango Wildlife Lodge
destinations.summary: Award-winning luxury safari lodge inside Save Valley Conservancy. 600 km² section, four luxury chalets and a tented camp.

destinations.name: Tugwi-Mukosi Dam Wall Viewpoint
destinations.summary: The 90.3 m concrete-face rock-fill dam wall — an engineering landmark with a planned game park in its buffer zone.

destinations.name: Bangala Dam
destinations.summary: Major irrigation dam in Chivi/Zaka. Fishing, picnics, and a quiet alternative to Mutirikwi.

destinations.name: Muzhwi Dam
destinations.summary: Substantial Chivi dam used for irrigation and fishing. Smallholder irrigation revitalisation hub.

destinations.name: Chivi Mountain Ranges
destinations.summary: Beautiful ranges from Chibi-Turn-off through Madzivire communal lands to Runde Bridge. Mountain-tourism potential.

images.alt: Hills and rocky landscape in southern Zimbabwe

destinations.name: Runde-Tende River Confluence (Chasiyatende)
destinations.summary: Site of the planned Tende-Runde Dam (will be 2.5× Tugwi-Mukosi when built). Currently a dramatic mountain-and-river junction.

destinations.name: Chivi National Monument
destinations.summary: Cultural heritage stone site in Chivi District. Less-visited counterpart to Great Zimbabwe.

destinations.name: Ngundu
destinations.summary: Highway junction town on the A4 to Beitbridge — the gateway from Masvingo to Tugwi-Mukosi and Chiredzi.

destinations.name: Nerumedzo Sacred Mountain & Forest
destinations.summary: National monument and 12 km² sacred Jiri forest. Home of the seasonal Harurwa (edible stink bug) ceremonies, April–August. Rainmaking shrine.
destinations.longDescription: Nerumedzo is the spiritual heart of Bikita. Local tradition tells of Nemeso, the four-eyed founding ancestor who was given the harurwa stink bugs as a perpetual gift from the ancestors. Indigenous plant species inside the forest are protected by traditional law, making Nerumedzo a remarkable example of indigenous-knowledge conservation.

destinations.name: Chibvumani Ruins
destinations.summary: Dry-stone walled site (300+ stones) in Mamutse, Bikita. Part of the broader Zimbabwe Culture, dated by Caton-Thompson (1929).

destinations.name: Bikita Mountain (Mamutse)
destinations.summary: Sacred mountain after which the district is named. The original colonial offices were located in its foothills.

destinations.name: Devuli Ranch
destinations.summary: Game park and ranch in Bikita, near Save Valley Conservancy. Home of Chishakwe Lodge.

destinations.name: Devuli Range / Bikita Hills
destinations.summary: Series of low-lying hills with spiritual significance. Includes Bikita Mt. and Chirivira Mt.

destinations.name: Ruvuravingwe Mountain
destinations.summary: The highest and most prominent mountain in Bikita (out of 218 named peaks).

destinations.name: Siya Dam (Siye)
destinations.summary: Major Bikita dam, popular for fish breeding (bream).

destinations.name: Rozva Dam
destinations.summary: Significant Bikita dam used for fishing and irrigation.

destinations.name: Bikita Minerals Mine
destinations.summary: World's largest petalite (lithium) deposit — about 11 million tonnes. Industrial-tourism interest, owned by Sinomine since 2022.

destinations.name: Save Conservatives
destinations.summary: Big Five conservation area owned by Bikita Rural District Council. Includes the Mkonto gold mine.

destinations.name: Mashoko Mission
destinations.summary: Renowned Church of Christ mission with a famous hospital and high school. A beacon of education in the Matsai community.

destinations.name: Silveira Mission
destinations.summary: Historic Catholic mission station and high school. Stop on the way to Nerumedzo Sacred Forest.

destinations.name: Pamushana High School / Mission
destinations.summary: Dutch Reformed mission and high school with scenic grounds.

destinations.name: Nyika Growth Point
destinations.summary: Bikita District administrative centre. Local market and stop-over town.

destinations.name: Masvingo Provincial Museum
destinations.summary: Houses the original Great Zimbabwe Bird soapstone carvings and regional artefacts.

destinations.name: Old Fort National Monument
destinations.summary: Built in 1891 in the centre of Masvingo town as part of the colonial route from Salisbury (Harare) southward. Marks Masvingo as Zimbabwe's oldest colonial settlement.

destinations.name: Mushandike Sanctuary
destinations.summary: Game sanctuary west of Masvingo. Antelope, zebra, leopard. Beautiful Mopane forest in autumn colours.

destinations.name: Mushandike Dam
destinations.summary: Dam inside Mushandike Sanctuary — fishing and picnics.

destinations.name: Pokoteke Gorge
destinations.summary: Where the Pokoteke River cuts through the Beza Range north of Lake Mutirikwi. Hiking and scenery.

destinations.name: Sikato Bay (Lake Mutirikwi)
destinations.summary: Boat-cruise launch point on Lake Mutirikwi. Thunder Boat Cruises operates from here.

images.alt: Lake Mutirikwi shore at Sikato Bay

destinations.name: Kyle Dam Wall
destinations.summary: Walkable dam wall with sweeping views over Lake Mutirikwi. Built 1958–1961.

images.alt: Kyle Dam wall and Lake Mutirikwi

destinations.name: Mabudziya Village
destinations.summary: Well-preserved traditional Shona village. Authentic huts, dance, storytelling, and craft.

destinations.name: Takaindisa Village
destinations.summary: Traditional cultural village experience similar to Mabudziya — quieter and more local.

destinations.name: Charles Austin Theatre
destinations.summary: Local theatre and performing-arts venue at Masvingo Civic Centre.

destinations.name: Mucheke Stadium
destinations.summary: Home of Masvingo United FC. Local football culture and atmosphere.

destinations.name: Great Zimbabwe University
destinations.summary: One of Masvingo's two universities. Modern campus with cultural and heritage programmes.

destinations.name: Margaretha Hugo School for the Blind (Copota)
destinations.summary: Zimbabwe's first school for the visually impaired. Historic institution with a Reformed Church heritage.

destinations.name: Copota / Shakashe Dam Wall
destinations.summary: Quiet local picnic and nature spot just outside Masvingo city.

destinations.name: Sundowners Garden
destinations.summary: Tranquil garden retreat in Masvingo — quiet escape, popular for events.

destinations.name: Goshen Ranch
destinations.summary: Recreational ranch offering relaxed outdoor activities and game viewing.

destinations.name: Pamawiro
destinations.summary: Scenic confluence of the Chivaka and Shange rivers — peaceful and photogenic.

destinations.name: Monga Mountain
destinations.summary: Hiking and viewpoint location near Masvingo.

destinations.name: Sikato Lion Park
destinations.summary: Lion sanctuary near Lake Mutirikwi.

destinations.name: Nemanwa Growth Point
destinations.summary: Closest growth point to Great Zimbabwe — local market, lodgings, and crafts.

destinations.name: Njelele Shrine (Masvingo Province)
destinations.summary: Sacred rainmaking and ancestral-worship site (distinct from the more famous Matobo Njelele).

destinations.name: Manyuchi Dam (Lake Manyuchi)
destinations.summary: Built in 1974 on the Mwenezi River. Picnics, fishing, sunset views. 4×4 recommended due to road conditions.

destinations.name: Nuanetsi Ranch
destinations.summary: Massive cattle and game ranch in Mwenezi. Site of a planned ethanol plant.

destinations.name: Mwenezana Estate
destinations.summary: Sugar-related agricultural estate fed by Manyuchi Dam water.

destinations.name: Matibi 2 Communal Lands
destinations.summary: Cultural and rural baobab country — authentic Mwenezi communal landscape.

destinations.name: Rutenga
destinations.summary: Junction town and railway/road landmark in Mwenezi.

destinations.name: Mwenezi River Valley
destinations.summary: Scenic river course feeding Manyuchi Dam — birdlife and quiet riverside camps.

destinations.name: Serima Mission
destinations.summary: Famous Catholic mission with the Serima sculpture and woodcarving school. Internationally known for African Christian art and the Stations of the Cross.

destinations.name: Mupandawana (Gutu Town)
destinations.summary: Recently granted town status. Administrative centre of Gutu District.

destinations.name: Gutu Mission
destinations.summary: Historic mission station and school — peaceful highveld grounds.

destinations.name: Driefontein Mission Area
destinations.summary: Mission and farming country in Gutu. Quiet rural detour off the Masvingo–Mvuma road.

destinations.name: Manjirenji Dam
destinations.summary: Built 1964–1966 to supply Hippo Valley irrigation. Fishing, picnics, tourism on the rise.

destinations.name: Manjirenji Recreational Park
destinations.summary: Park surrounding Manjirenji Dam — quieter alternative to Mutirikwi.

destinations.name: Jerera Growth Point
destinations.summary: Zaka District administrative centre and market town.

destinations.name: Ndanga (Historical District Seat)
destinations.summary: Historic former district seat with cultural and historical interest.

destinations.name: Harurwa Season Experience (Nerumedzo)
destinations.summary: Annual harvest of edible stink bugs (April–August) — a unique cultural-culinary experience preceded by ancestral ceremonies.

destinations.name: Great Limpopo Cultural Trade Fair
destinations.summary: Annual cross-border cultural fair (Zimbabwe / Mozambique / South Africa) at Boli Mhlanguleni since 2013.

images.alt: Cultural festival dancers in southern Africa

destinations.name: Mkonto Mine
destinations.summary: Council-owned gold belt at Save Conservatives. Industrial-tourism interest.

destinations.name: Chilonga Irrigation Scheme
destinations.summary: Working agricultural landscape — citrus and crop fields, important irrigation engineering.

destinations.name: Tshovani Suburb (Chiredzi)
destinations.summary: Sugar-industry community life. Lively suburb tied to Hippo Valley Estates.

destinations.name: Chiredzi Wildlife Area
destinations.summary: Broader savannah conservation zone (~5,000 km²) supporting elephants, antelopes, and the wider Lowveld ecosystem.

itineraries.title: Heritage Half-Day
itineraries.description: Great Zimbabwe + Lake Mutirikwi + Masvingo Museum — perfect introduction to Masvingo's headline sights.

itineraries.title: Lake & Dam Loop
itineraries.description: Mutirikwi to Mushandike to Tugwi-Mukosi — a full-day tour of Masvingo's biggest waters.

itineraries.title: Lowveld Safari
itineraries.description: Chiredzi base camp, Gonarezhou with Chilojo Cliffs, Save Valley, and Malilangwe — 3 to 5 days, 4×4 essential.

itineraries.title: Cultural Bikita
itineraries.description: Nerumedzo Sacred Forest, Chibvumani Ruins, and the Mashoko/Silveira missions — a deep cultural day.

itineraries.title: Mwenezi Escape
itineraries.description: Manyuchi Dam and the Nuanetsi ranchlands — quiet, remote, baobab country. 4×4 recommended.

itineraries.title: Gutu Art Detour
itineraries.description: Serima Mission carvings — an under-visited gem for art lovers, on the way north.

itineraries.title: Zaka Quiet Fishing Day
itineraries.description: Manjirenji Dam and the surrounding Zaka country — calm and budget-friendly.
